IPO Fever and the Cost of the Crowd

Borsa Istanbul 2020–2025 · Underpricing, Herding & Inflation · Dokuz Eylul University

Research Question

Main RQ: To what extent is IPO underpricing in Borsa Istanbul explained by valuation signals and investor behavior during the 2020–2025 IPO boom?
Sub-Question 1
Valuation Ratios
Do valuation ratios (P/E and P/B) influence IPO underpricing?
Sub-Question 2
Investor Herding
Is there statistical evidence of investor herding during IPO trading periods?
Sub-Question 3
Market Conditions
Do market conditions (BIST performance, inflation, CPI) affect IPO underpricing outcomes?

Theoretical Framework

Information Asymmetry Theory

IPO underpricing arises due to asymmetric information between investors and issuers. Informed investors can better assess true value, forcing issuers to underprice to attract uninformed participants (Rock, 1986; Benveniste & Spindt, 1989).

Behavioral Finance

Investor sentiment and herd behavior can amplify price movements, especially in emerging markets with a high share of retail investors. Overreaction and speculative trading may explain extreme underpricing (De Bondt & Thaler, 1985; CCK, 2000).

Context: Borsa Istanbul provides a unique environment where both mechanisms may operate simultaneously. SPK enforcement actions during 2020–2025 serve as evidence of a speculative trading environment in which retail investor sentiment may amplify IPO price movements.

Variable Structure

Dependent Variable:

IPO Underpricing (tavan serisi)

Key Independent Variables:

Price-to-Book (P/B) Price-to-Earnings (P/E) Herding Indicator (CSAD)

Control Variables:

Market Capitalization BIST Index Performance Inflation Rate Consumer Price Index (CPI)

Data Sources & Methodology

DataSourcePeriodNotes
IPO Datahalkarz.com + KAP + SPK2020-2025209 IPOs, 3-source verification
Daily PricesYahoo Finance2020-2025Split-adjusted close prices
CPI (TÜFE)TÜÍK (Turkish Statistical Institute)2019-2025Base year 2003=100, monthly
BIST-100 IndexYahoo Finance (XU100.IS)2020-2025Daily closing values
USD/TRYYahoo Finance (USDTRY=X)2020-2025Daily exchange rates
SPK PenaltiesSPK Weekly Bulletins2020-202547 verified cases
CPI Base Year Explanation: TÜÍK publishes the Consumer Price Index (TÜFE) with base year 2003=100. A value of 3514 in December 2025 means prices are 35.1x higher than in 2003. This differs from Trading Economics or World Bank data which rebase to different years (e.g. 2015=100 or 2020=100). For our inflation calculations, the base year does not matter because we compute period-specific inflation as (CPIend / CPIstart) − 1, which yields the same result regardless of base year. Source: TÜÍK EVDS, Series: “Tüketici Fiyat Endeksi, 2003=100” (data.tuik.gov.tr).

IPO Underpricing Analysis

SQ1: Do valuation ratios (P/E and P/B) influence IPO underpricing?

Tavan Serisi (Consecutive Limit-Up): BIST imposes ±10% daily price limits. When an IPO hits the upper limit on day 1 and continues for multiple days, conventional first-day returns severely understate true underpricing. We measure from IPO close to first free-trading day.

Statistical Tests for Underpricing > 0:
One-sample t-test: t = 10.824, p = 7.55e-22 (N = 204)
Wilcoxon signed-rank: W = 0, p = 3.15e-24 (N = 137, excludes zeros)
Conclusion: IPO underpricing is statistically significant at the 1% level under both parametric and non-parametric tests.

Multi-Period Returns

Contrarian Strategy: Post-Tavan Returns

After the tavan streak ends, do stocks revert? A contrarian SELL at 5 days earns −3.3% (p=0.021), while long-term (365d) returns are +72.6%.

Market Environment: SPK Enforcement

Regulatory enforcement as evidence of speculative trading conditions

Turkey's Capital Markets Board (SPK) actively prosecutes market manipulation. Rather than treating manipulation as a primary explanatory variable, we interpret these enforcement cases as evidence of a market environment in which speculative trading and retail investor sentiment may amplify IPO price movements. Event study: MacKinlay (1997) with Boehmer (1991) standardized test.

Herding Analysis & Inflation

SQ2: Evidence of herding? · SQ3: Market conditions & inflation effects

CSAD Herding Test (CCK 2000)

Following Chang, Cheng & Khorana (2000), we test for herding via: CSAD = α + γ₁|Rm| + γ₂Rm² + ε. A significantly negative γ₂ indicates herding.
γ₂
0.645
t-statistic
0.432
p-value
0.666
Result: No evidence of herding (γ₂ = 0.645, p = 0.666). The positive coefficient suggests return dispersion increases MORE than proportionally during extreme market movements, inconsistent with herding. Note: Zhang (2024) shows CSAD test power averages only 59.37%.

Full CSAD Regression Results

VariableCoefficientInterpretation
α (Intercept)0.013923Baseline cross-sectional dispersion
γ₁ (|Rm|)0.106388Linear sensitivity to market return
γ₂ (Rm²)0.645011Non-linear term (negative = herding)
R²0.0960Proportion of variance explained
Adj. R²0.0948Adjusted for degrees of freedom
N observations1496Daily observations (BIST-100 stocks)

Inflation Illusion

Fisher Equation: Real Return = (1 + Nominal) / (1 + Inflation) − 1. Turkey's extreme inflation (peaking at 85.5% YoY in Oct 2022) means nominal IPO gains vastly overstate real wealth creation.
BIST-100 Nominal
+711%
2020-2025
CPI Increase
+687%
Purchasing power lost
BIST-100 Real
+3%
CPI-adjusted
BIST-100 in USD
+21%
Dollar terms
The Inflation Illusion: BIST-100 gained +711% in nominal terms but only +3% in real (CPI-adjusted) terms. In dollar terms, the gain was +21%. The gap between nominal and real returns is the "Inflation Illusion."

Source: TÜÍK (Turkish Statistical Institute), Consumer Price Index (TÜFE), Base Year 2003=100. Values represent the cumulative price level relative to 2003, not percentage changes. For example, 3514 means prices are 35.1x what they were in 2003. Data: data.tuik.gov.tr | TCMB EVDS: evds2.tcmb.gov.tr

BIST-100 vs Inflation vs USD/TRY

All three series normalized to 100 at January 2020, allowing direct comparison of growth rates. If BIST-100 (blue) is below CPI (red), equities have lost real value.

Nominal vs Real IPO Returns

Real returns computed per-IPO using the Fisher equation with daily-interpolated CPI data from TUIK. Each IPO's inflation is measured over its specific holding period.

PeriodAvg Nominal ReturnAvg Period InflationAvg Real Return
Tavan Series67.8%1.2%66.6%
30 Days51.0%5.2%45.8%

Illusion Zone: 1-Year Returns

Each dot is one IPO. The red shaded area is the "Illusion Zone" — IPOs with positive nominal returns but negative real returns. These investors thought they were profiting, but actually lost purchasing power.
Money Illusion Cases: 48/198 IPOs (24%) had positive nominal but NEGATIVE real 1-year returns.

Does Inflation Drive IPO Demand?

Modigliani & Cohn (1979) Hypothesis: Investors in high-inflation environments use nominal rates to discount, making stocks appear more attractive, potentially driving more IPO demand.
Pearson r = 0.195 (p = 0.1537) — Not significant at 5% level. No statistically significant relationship between monthly inflation and IPO frequency.

Conclusions & Statistical Results

IPO Fever and the Cost of the Crowd · Borsa Istanbul 2020–2025

Main RQ: To what extent is IPO underpricing in Borsa Istanbul explained by valuation signals and investor behavior during the 2020–2025 IPO boom?

The empirical results provide partial support for this hypothesis. Valuation indicators—particularly the price-to-book ratio—show a statistically significant relationship with IPO underpricing. However, the analysis finds limited statistical evidence for systematic herding behavior among investors during IPO trading periods.

SQ1: Do Valuation Ratios Influence Underpricing? Partial

IPO underpricing is confirmed: Mean = 67.8% (t = 10.82, p < 0.001), Median = 33.1%. Wilcoxon test also significant (W = 0, p < 0.001). N = 209 IPOs.

Cross-Sectional Regression: 4-Model Comparison

We estimate four progressively enriched OLS specifications to identify determinants of IPO underpricing. Only Model 4 (Log-level) is jointly significant (F-test p < 0.01).
ModelNR²Adj. R²F-statF p-valueSignificant?
Basic2040.09690.06474.6600.0001Yes
Fundamentals1120.19290.11301.5390.1367No
Full1120.21760.10471.2380.2615No
Log-level1120.21440.13672.6810.0060Yes

yr_2025-0.21000.3416-0.6150.5386
hot_market0.06280.08430.7450.4562
Result: Price-to-book ratio is the only significant determinant (p = 0.040). P/E ratio (p > 0.50) and market cap (p = 0.095) are not significant. Observable fundamentals explain only 10–21% of variation, suggesting underpricing is primarily behavioural.

SQ2: Is There Evidence of Investor Herding? Not Found

No significant herding detected: γ₂ = 0.645, p = 0.666. The positive coefficient suggests return dispersion increases during extreme market movements, inconsistent with herding. This null result must be qualified by low test power (Zhang 2024: avg 59.37%).

SQ3: Do Market Conditions Affect Underpricing? Partial

SPK Enforcement: CAAR = -3.8% over [-30,+30] window. 37/45 (82.2%) individual stocks show significant negative CARs. SPK enforcement actions confirm the speculative market environment.
Post-Tavan Dynamics: Short-term mean reversion at 5 days (−3.3%, p = 0.021). Long-term returns positive (+72.6% at 365d). Consistent with De Bondt & Thaler (1985) overreaction hypothesis.
Inflation Illusion: BIST-100 gained +711% nominally but only +3% in real terms. 48/198 IPOs (24%) had positive nominal but negative real 1-year returns.

Summary of Findings

IPO pricing in Borsa Istanbul is influenced primarily by valuation-related signals (particularly P/B), while behavioral factors appear to play a secondary role. The extreme underpricing (67.8% mean) cannot be fully explained by observable firm characteristics (R² = 10–21%), pointing to unobserved behavioural and institutional factors unique to Turkey's price-limit regime.
Sub-QuestionFindingStatistical SupportVerdict
SQ1: Valuation RatiosP/B significant (p=0.040), P/E not significant (p>0.50)R²=21.4% (Model 4)Partial
SQ2: Investor HerdingNo herding detected via CSADγ₂=0.645, p=0.666Not Found
SQ3: Market ConditionsOverreaction confirmed; inflation erodes gains5d: p=0.021, 365d: p<0.001Partial
IPO Fever and the Cost of the Crowd
Dokuz Eylul University · Faculty of Business · 2026
All statistics computed from verified data sources. No hardcoded values.
Data: TÜÍK (CPI) · Yahoo Finance (prices) · halkarz.com + KAP + SPK (IPO data) · SPK Weekly Bulletins (penalties)
